source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A deep-sea diver is suspended beneath the surface of Loch Ness by a cable that is attached to a boat on the surface (\textbf{figure}). The diver and his suit have a volume of \( 0.0800\,\text{m}^3 \). The cable has a diameter of \( 2.00\,\text{cm} \) and a linear mass density of \( \mu = 1.10\,\text{kg/m} \). The diver thinks he sees something moving in the murky depths and jerks the end of the cable back and forth to send transverse waves up the cable as a signal to his companions in the boat. What is the tension in the cable at its lower end, where it is attached to the diver?
